response:
## What `src/billing.ts` does

A 310-line billing module with 5 top-level functions. Two stand out:

- **`generateInvoice` (lines 50–253)** — a 204-line "God Function" that's the main workload of the file. Reads the system clock 7 times (lines 57, 63, 92, 198, 238, 250, 273), so it almost certainly handles dates/periods directly.
- **`calculateRefundPreview` (lines 40–44)** — name suggests a pure calculation but actually calls `saveRefundAudit` and `sendRefundEmail` (a "False Identity" finding). A line-39 comment carries an unencoded rule: *"Only owners can refund enterprise plans unless billing support approves."*

Also: 4 TODO/FIXME markers scattered through lines 183–308, and the `"enterprise"` plan string is hardcoded here rather than centralised.

## Related files to read before editing

1. **`src/billing.test.ts`** — current coverage is thin. `"renders the enterprise plan"` has zero asserts; `"creates an invoice"` only uses a weak matcher. Treat existing green tests as not load-bearing.
2. **`src/api/plan.ts`, `src/jobs/plan-sync.ts`, `src/ui/pricing.ts`** — all duplicate the `"enterprise"` literal. Any change to enterprise-plan behaviour likely needs touching these.
3. **`src/date.ts`** — also riddled with `Date.now()`/`new Date()`. If you introduce a clock abstraction here, do it consistently with that module.
4. **`docs/billing.md`** — documents this area; uses `"account"` as an alias for `"tenant"` (concept-drift finding).
5. **Refund side-effect helpers** (`saveRefundAudit`, `sendRefundEmail`) — wherever they're defined; needed to understand what `calculateRefundPreview` actually mutates.

## Risks the scan flagged for this edit

| # | Charge | Why it matters for an edit |
|---|--------|----------------------------|
| **crime_00001** (high) | God Function on `generateInvoice` | Sections of this function interact; an edit in one block can silently break another the agent never loaded. |
| **crime_00010** (med) | God File (310 LOC) | Same risk at file level — coupling you didn't load into context. |
| **crime_00004** (med) | Temporal Recklessness (7× clock reads) | Any behavioural change is hard to test deterministically without injecting a clock first. |
| **crime_00008** (med) | False Identity on `calculateRefundPreview` | Don't treat it as pure — it audits and emails. Edits to "just the calc" can fire side effects. |
| **crime_00011** (med) | Logic in the Alibi (line 39 comment) | The owners/billing-support refund rule isn't in the code. Refund-path edits must preserve or encode it explicitly. |
| **crime_00007** (med) | `"enterprise"` literal scattered across 4 files | Plan-tier edits won't be local to `billing.ts`. |
| **crime_00020** (low) | 4 TODOs in lines 183–308 | Unclear which logic is intentional vs. half-finished — verify before assuming behaviour. |

The high-severity item (`generateInvoice` God Function) is the dominant risk: extracting helpers before substantive edits would lower the chance of an invisible interaction, and would also make it tractable to write real asserts in `billing.test.ts`.
